ground_truth.description: focusOnBounds() duplicates the camera positioning logic from fitToView() (line 189) and flyToEntity() (line 220). The center/size calculation, FOV distance computation, and camera+controls update are identical across all three functions. The existing functions should be refactored to call focusOnBounds() instead of duplicating the same 12 lines of camera math.

focusOnEntities() is nearly identical to fitToView() — both compute a bounding box from meshes and position the camera. The only difference is fitToView uses all meshes while focusOnEntities uses a subset. fitToView could be implemented as focusOnEntities([...getMeshes().keys()]).

focusOnBounds() applies a padding multiplier (default 1.2) to maxDim before computing camera distance, while the existing fitToView() and flyToEntity() do not. This means focusOnBounds-based views will have 20% more whitespace around the target than direct calls, creating inconsistent zoom behavior.
